messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following smart home device privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"SmartHome Hub collects voice commands, device usage patterns, and home network information to operate your smart home devices. Voice recordings are processed in real time and not stored. Usage patterns are retained for 12 months to improve automation. We may share anonymised usage data with product development partners. Our servers are located in Germany. Contact privacy@smarthome.io for data requests."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [FAIL] No lawful basis stated for any processing activity.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Voice recordings can capture biometric data (voice print) and reveal health or religious information. The claim that recordings are not stored does not eliminate the risk during real-time processing. No safeguards are mentioned.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Device operation, automation improvement, and product development are each stated as purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Home network information collection is not justified. The extent of usage pattern collection is not defined.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] 12-month retention for usage patterns stated. Retention for home network information is not addressed.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [FAIL] Only a contact email is provided. No specific rights are listed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PARTIAL] Product development partners referenced as recipients of anonymised data. Partners not named; anonymisation standard not described.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] Germany-based servers confirm EEA processing.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed. Large-scale processing of in-home behavioural and voice data may require a DPO.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (no lawful basis), Art. 9 (biometric voice data risk unaddressed), Art. 5 (home network data retention unstated), Art. 15-21 (rights not listed), Art. 37 (DPO not addressed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State lawful basis for each processing activity
- Acknowledge and address biometric risk in real-time voice processing
- Justify home network data collection or remove it
- Add retention period for home network data
- List all data subject rights explicitly
- Name product development partners or confirm anonymisation meets GDPR standard
- Assess and appoint DPO if required
- Add breach notification commitment
